does: This paper presents an updated framework for identifying and mitigating severe harms that could arise from frontier artificial intelligence systems. The work provides concrete methodologies for measuring AI capabilities that pose risks, along with corresponding evaluation and protection protocols. The framework articulates key risk dimensions—such as autonomous action capacity, self-improvement potential, and deceptive behavior—and establishes thresholds at which protective measures should be triggered. It combines technical assessments with governance structures to guide safe development of advanced AI systems.
connects: This work builds on growing literature examining existential and severe risks from advanced AI, extending earlier safety research that focused on alignment, interpretability, and capability control. It responds to the challenge of making AI risk assessment more concrete and measurable, addressing limitations in prior frameworks that lacked specificity about which capabilities warrant intervention. The research synthesizes approaches from AI safety, governance studies, and risk analysis traditions, incorporating insights from both academic research and practitioner experience in industry labs developing frontier models.
enables: This framework sets up more rigorous testing and governance procedures for organizations developing the most capable AI systems, potentially becoming a standard for responsible development practices across the field. It enables regulators and other AI developers to establish comparable evaluation benchmarks and intervention protocols, facilitating coordination on severe risk mitigation. The work opens paths toward institutionalizing preparedness measures that scale with AI capability advances, supporting both internal governance at labs and external oversight mechanisms that can adapt as frontier systems evolve.
